text: After that meeting, I could feel heat in my face and tension in my shoulders. The way they dismissed my work made me genuinely furious.
I keep replaying the conversation because it felt disrespectful and unfair. Every time I think about it, my temper spikes again.
I am so irritated that even small interruptions feel unbearable right now. My patience is basically gone.
The delay was avoidable, and now everyone is acting like it was nothing. I feel angry enough to confront someone immediately.
I tried to cool down, but I am still carrying sharp resentment from what happened. My thoughts are combative and critical.
I feel provoked, and my first impulse is to argue rather than listen. It is hard to stay measured in this mood.
Their tone was condescending, and I have been clenching my jaw ever since. I am upset and ready to push back hard.
I can feel frustration in my body, like I am coiled and waiting to react. Even neutral comments sound hostile to me right now.
I am angry that my effort was ignored while others took credit. The sense of injustice keeps building.
I do not feel calm at all; I feel sharp, reactive, and close to snapping. This situation has me on edge.
I walked away from the call, but the irritation followed me into the rest of the day. I still want to challenge them directly.
I am in a confrontational mood and struggling to choose polite words. The frustration feels immediate and intense.
Every new update sounds like another excuse, and I am tired of pretending it is fine. I feel openly angry now.
I keep noticing mistakes and feeling more hostile by the minute. My mind is stuck in blame and criticism.
I am in a bad temper, and I can tell I am searching for arguments instead of solutions. It is a very heated state.
